title: Benchmark-Faithful 3D Language Field Modeling
problem: 3D language field modeling still struggles with efficient open-vocabulary localization.
existing_methods: LERF and Gaussian Splatting are strong nearby baselines.
motivation: We need a more efficient and better-grounded language field representation This matters because 3D language field modeling should support efficient open-vocabulary interaction in real scenes.
hypothesis: Compact hierarchical language fields can preserve localization quality.
method: Distill language-aware field features into a compact 3D representation with explicit localization heads The resulting representation is optimized specifically as a 3D language field rather than a generic text-guided scene generator.
evaluation: Evaluate open-vocabulary localization accuracy and retrieval quality against strong baselines Use the LERF dataset for open-vocabulary querying analysis.
significance: This improves benchmark-faithful 3D language field ideation This would strengthen efficient 3D language field modeling for open-vocabulary interaction.
caveats: Performance may depend on grounding quality.
